name: Wano Country
description: Wano Country is an isolationist nation inspired by feudal Japan, known for its samurai warriors, traditional culture, and rich history. The country is naturally protected by waterfalls and has been closed off from the outside world for centuries. It's the homeland of powerful samurai and the Kozuki Clan.
history: Wano was once a prosperous nation ruled by the Kozuki Clan, master stonemasons who created the Poneglyphs. Twenty years before the main story, the country was taken over by Kurozumi Orochi with the help of Kaido and the Beast Pirates, leading to the execution of Kozuki Oden. The land was divided into regions, with the Flower Capital remaining prosperous while other regions suffered. The Kozuki retainers were scattered, and Oden's children went into hiding. The country was eventually liberated by the alliance of the Straw Hat Pirates, Heart Pirates, Kid Pirates, and the samurai of Wano in a massive war against Kaido.
culture: Wano culture is deeply rooted in honor, loyalty, and the samurai code. The society is hierarchical with shoguns, daimyos, and samurai. Traditional arts like sword-making, blacksmithing, and crafts are highly valued. The country has its own language, customs, and isolationist policies. Festivals, bathhouses, and traditional entertainment are central to daily life. The concept of 'closing one's heart' and enduring hardship is prevalent.
transportation: Transportation includes walking, horseback riding, small boats in rivers, and palanquins for nobility. The regions are connected by roads and bridges. Climbing waterfalls via carp-swimming technique is used to enter the country.

traditions: Lavish feasts in the Flower Capital
quirks: Food scarcity in Kuri and Hakumai during Orochi's rule

notablePeople.name: Kozuki Oden
notablePeople.role: Former Daimyo of Kuri / Legendary Samurai
notablePeople.description: A legendary samurai who sailed with both Whitebeard and Gol D. Roger. His execution sparked the revolution 20 years later.

notablePeople.name: Kozuki Momonosuke
notablePeople.description: Oden's son who was sent forward in time. He becomes the new shogun after Wano's liberation.

notablePeople.name: Kozuki Hiyori
notablePeople.description: Oden's daughter who stayed in the present as the oiran Komurasaki, secretly supporting the rebellion. Inherited her father's sword Enma.

notablePeople.name: Roronoa Zoro
notablePeople.description: Luffy's first mate who played a crucial role in Wano's liberation and inherited Oden's sword Enma.

notablePeople.name: Kaido
notablePeople.role: Former Occupier / Yonko / Emperor of the Sea
notablePeople.description: The 'Strongest Creature' who occupied Wano for 20 years until defeated by Luffy in an epic battle.

notablePeople.name: King
notablePeople.description: The last surviving Lunarian and Kaido's strongest commander. Defeated by Zoro in an epic clash.

notablePeople.name: Queen
notablePeople.description: A cyborg All-Star obsessed with science and viruses. Former colleague of Judge and Vegapunk.

notablePeople.name: Kurozumi Orochi
notablePeople.description: The treacherous shogun who allied with Kaido to take over Wano through deception.

notablePeople.name: Kin'emon
notablePeople.description: Oden's loyal retainer who led the resistance and allied with the Straw Hats. Wields the Foxfire Style.

notablePeople.name: Denjiro
notablePeople.description: One of Oden's retainers who disguised himself as Kyoshiro, Orochi's bodyguard, to protect Hiyori and await the prophesied day.

notablePeople.name: Yamato
notablePeople.role: Kaido's Child / Oden Admirer / Guardian Deity
notablePeople.description: The oni child of Kaido who idolizes Oden and fought alongside the alliance to free Wano. Ate the Mythical Dog-Dog Fruit Model: Okuchi-no-Makami.

notablePeople.name: Tenguyama Hitetsu
notablePeople.description: A master swordsmith who is secretly Oden's father, the former shogun Kozuki Sukiyaki, in disguise.

notablePeople.name: Shimotsuki Yasuie
notablePeople.description: The beloved daimyo who sacrificed himself to give the alliance a fighting chance, spreading joy even in death.

notablePeople.name: Ashura Doji
notablePeople.description: A former retainer who became the bandit Shutenmaru after Oden's death. Sacrificed himself in the raid.

notablePeople.name: Kawamatsu
notablePeople.description: A fish-man samurai who protected Hiyori and was imprisoned for years. Expert in Fish-man Karate and swordsmanship.

notablePeople.name: Inuarashi
notablePeople.description: Duke of the Mokomo Dukedom and one of Oden's retainers. Ruler of Zou during the day.

notablePeople.name: Nekomamushi
notablePeople.description: Guardian of the Whale Forest and one of Oden's retainers. Ruler of Zou during the night.

notablePeople.name: Kiku (Kikunojo)
notablePeople.description: A gentle but deadly samurai retainer who fought valiantly in the raid on Onigashima.

notablePeople.name: Raizo
notablePeople.description: A ninja retainer who endured torture without betraying his comrades. Master of scrolls and ninjutsu.

notablePeople.name: Big Mom (Charlotte Linlin)
notablePeople.description: A Yonko who arrived in Wano seeking Luffy, temporarily allied with Kaido before fighting the alliance.

notablePeople.name: Hyogoro
notablePeople.description: The legendary yakuza boss who taught Luffy advanced Armament Haki in Udon prison.

subLocations.name: Flower Capital
subLocations.description: The prosperous capital of Wano where the shogun resides. Features traditional architecture, festivals, and entertainment districts. Remained wealthy during Orochi's rule while other regions suffered.

subLocations.name: Kuri Region
subLocations.description: Oden's former domain, once lawless but transformed by him. Contains Oden Castle (destroyed) and important sites in Wano's liberation.

subLocations.name: Udon Region
subLocations.description: A harsh mining region turned into a massive prisoner labor camp by Kaido. Where Luffy was imprisoned and trained his Haki.

subLocations.name: Onigashima
subLocations.description: Kaido's skull-shaped fortress island where the Beast Pirates were headquartered. Site of the climactic battle for Wano's freedom.

subLocations.name: Ringo Region
subLocations.description: A frozen northern region known for graves of legendary samurai. Where Zoro obtained the legendary sword Shusui.

subLocations.name: Hakumai Region
subLocations.description: The region containing Wano's main port. Former domain of Daimyo Yasuie, featuring traditional architecture and coastal access.

subLocations.name: Kibi Region
subLocations.description: An agricultural region that suffered under Orochi's rule, with polluted resources and poverty.

subLocations.name: Oden Castle
subLocations.description: Kozuki Oden's castle in Kuri, destroyed when Kaido attacked. Where Momonosuke and the retainers were sent to the future.

items.name: Enma
items.description: One of the only swords to ever wound Kaido. Originally Oden's sword, inherited by Zoro. Draws out the user's Haki.

items.name: Ame no Habakiri
items.description: Oden's other legendary sword, inherited by Momonosuke. One of the 21 Great Grade swords.

items.name: Seastone
items.description: A rare mineral found only in Wano that nullifies Devil Fruit powers. Used in weapons and restraints.

items.name: Oden's Journal
items.description: Oden's personal journal detailing his adventures with Whitebeard and Roger, containing crucial information about Laugh Tale.

items.name: Road Poneglyph
items.description: One of the four Road Poneglyphs needed to find Laugh Tale, hidden beneath Wano.

items.name: Nidai Kitetsu
items.description: The second of the Kitetsu cursed blade series, currently in Luffy's possession after being held by Hitetsu.

items.name: SMILE Fruits
items.description: Defective artificial Zoan fruits manufactured for Kaido's army. Only 10% work properly.

items.name: Wano's Sake
items.description: Traditional sake brewed in Wano, symbolizing celebration and brotherhood among samurai.

items.name: Flame Clouds
items.description: Mystical clouds created by dragon-type Devil Fruits, used by Kaido to lift and move Onigashima.

factions.name: Kozuki Clan
factions.description: The rightful rulers of Wano who were overthrown 20 years ago. Led the resistance and alliance that liberated the country.

factions.name: Beast Pirates (Defeated)
factions.description: A Yonko crew that occupied Wano for 20 years, exploiting its resources and people. Completely defeated in the Onigashima raid.

factions.name: Kurozumi Clan (Defeated)
factions.description: The traitorous clan that conspired with Kaido to usurp the shogunate through deceit and murder.

factions.name: Ninja-Pirate-Mink-Samurai Alliance
factions.description: The grand alliance formed to liberate Wano from Kaido and Orochi's tyranny. Over 5,000 warriors strong.

factions.name: Wano Samurai
factions.description: The traditional warrior class of Wano who joined the rebellion to restore honor and freedom to their country.

factions.name: Big Mom Pirates (Temporary Presence)
factions.description: A Yonko crew that arrived in Wano, temporarily allied with Kaido before being defeated by Law and Kid.

events: Fire Festival and raid on Onigashima
Rooftop battle against Kaido and Big Mom

quickFacts: Closed off from the world for over 800 years
Only source of Seastone in the world
Ruled by samurai code and honor

videos.title: Luffy Gear 5 Awakening
videos.description: The moment Luffy awakens his true Devil Fruit power and becomes the Sun God Nika.

videos.title: Zoro Conquers King
videos.description: Zoro's epic battle against King the Wildfire, mastering Enma in the process.

videos.title: Oden's Legendary Hour
videos.description: The tragic but inspiring tale of Kozuki Oden's final moments.
